images.caption: Figure 2:Overview of GEAR.(a)The conventional pipeline freezes a pretrained VQ-VAE and trains the AR model alone with the next-token-prediction (NTP) and REPA losses.(b)Naively making the pipeline end-to-end by passing AR gradients back into the tokenizer through the straight-through estimator (STE, drawn as thezigzag arrows↝\rightsquigarrow) is highly unstable and collapses (cf.table˜7).(c)GEAR reads the per-position assignment both as ahard(one-hot) and asoft(temperature-scaled) matrix: the hard branch carries NTP and the hard REPA loss to update only the AR model, while the differentiable soft branch carries a REPA loss that bypasses the upper AR blocks and flows back (the dashed arrow⇢\dashrightarrow) to update only the tokenizer, giving a stable end-to-end guidance signal.

Figure 3:Training dynamics on GPIC.NTP loss (a) and REPA alignment loss (b) over the single epoch (390390k steps). We train an AR onGEAR’s end-to-end-improved tokenizer and on theoriginaltokenizer. Both are kept frozen here, so the two runs differ only in tokenizer quality. To reach the baseline’s final loss, GEAR is2.5×2.5\timesfaster on NTP and11.1×11.1\timesfaster on REPA alignment.

Figure 4:Codebook usage during end-to-end fine-tuning.Over GEAR’s joint fine-tuning on ImageNet (τ=0.1\tau{=}0.1), we track the cumulative top-11/1010assignment mass (a–c), the usage entropy in nats (d), and the effective codebook sizeexp⁡(H)\exp(H)(e). The blue dashed line marks the warm-up tokenizer, the start of fine-tuning that LlamaGen-REPA uses. The distribution sharpens rapidly, peaks in concentration near3030k steps, then relaxes and converges (after∼120{\sim}120k) to a state more concentrated and lower-entropy than the warm-up.

Figure 5:AR-feature alignment to DINOv2 across depth.GEAR-Lvs.LlamaGen-REPA-L, both aligning the88-th layer to DINOv2 (gray band) and trained for400400k steps. The horizontal axis is the AR layer index, where layer0is the output of the AR embedding layer. We take therawper-layer hidden states (before the REPA projection head) and measure alignment to DINOv2 via CKNNA[18]and CKA[19]at the image level (a,b) and patch level (d,e), together with Moran’sII[27](c) and the FFT high-frequency ratio (f). The gray dashed line is DINOv2’s own value, and the shaded bands denote±1\pm 1standard deviation. The two models are comparable at the image level, butGEARis markedly closer to DINOv2 at the patch level (d,e) and in both locality statistics (c,f), showing that end-to-end guidance yields features with stronger local, spatially-causal structure.
